Package calendar.event

Class EventSeries

  • java.lang.Object
    • calendar.event.EventSeries
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    EventInterface
    public class EventSeries
extends java.lang.Object
implements EventInterface
    This class represents a series of recurring calendar events, and generates the individual event keys based on weekdays, occurrences/end date.

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Class Description
      static class  EventSeries.EventSeriesBuilder
      Builder class for creating and reset instances.

      Retrieves the end date of this series.

      Get a copy of the current set of event keys.

      To get the event status of the event series.

      Retrieves the date of the first event in the series.

      Retrieves the number of occurrences of this series.

      Retrieves the set of keys for this series.

      To get the start date/time of the event series.

      Retrieves the recurrence string (e.g., "MWR").

      Checks if the series is defined as an all-day event.

    • Method Detail

      • builder

        public static EventSeries.EventSeriesBuilder builder​(java.lang.String subject,
                                                     java.time.LocalDateTime start,
                                                     java.lang.String weekdays)
        Factory method to obtain a new builder instance.
        Parameters:
        subject - the subject of the event series
        start - the start date and time of the first occurrence
        weekdays - a string representing the recurrence days (e.g., "MWR")
        Returns:
        a new EventSeriesBuilder instance

      • toBuilder

        public EventSeries.EventSeriesBuilder toBuilder()
        Creates a new builder initialized with the current EventSeries's data. For modifying an existing series.
        Returns:
        a EventSeriesBuilder with given details

      • generateKeys

        public java.util.Set<EventKey> generateKeys()
        Generates the set of EventKey objects for all events in the series.
        Returns:
        an unmodifiable set of EventKeys
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - if no events are found

      • markDateRemoved

        public void markDateRemoved​(java.time.LocalDate date)
        Marks a date removed from key generation.
        Parameters:
        date - the date to be removed from the series

      • getSeriesId

        public java.lang.String getSeriesId()
        Description copied from interface: EventInterface
        To get the series id of the event series.
        Specified by:
        getSeriesId in interface EventInterface
        Returns:
        the series id of the event series

      • getEventKeys

        public java.util.Set<EventKey> getEventKeys()
        Get a copy of the current set of event keys.
        Returns:
        a copy of the set of EventKeys

      • removeEventKey

        public void removeEventKey​(EventKey eventKey)
        Removes an EventKey from the series keys.
        Parameters:
        eventKey - the key to remove

      • getSeriesKeys

        public java.util.Set<EventKey> getSeriesKeys()
        Retrieves the set of keys for this series.
        Returns:
        an unmodifiable set of EventKey instances

      • getRemovedDates

        public java.util.Set<java.time.LocalDate> getRemovedDates()
        Retrieves the set of dates that have been removed.
        Returns:
        an unmodifiable set of removed dates

        public java.time.LocalDate getFirstOccurrence()
        Retrieves the date of the first event in the series.
        Returns:

      • getWeekdays

        public java.lang.String getWeekdays()
        Retrieves the recurrence string (e.g., "MWR").

        public java.lang.Integer getOccurrences()
        Retrieves the number of occurrences of this series.

        Retrieves the end date of this series.
        Returns:

        Checks if the series is defined as an all-day event.
